column1: LOHIYA OUTLET AI — KIRANA UNIFIED SURVEY + INTERVIEW FLOW

Suggested visit flow: 1) Profile the outlet & operating model  →  2) Check digital / POS readiness and current bookkeeping  →  3) Understand customer demand, wishlist, and purchase sourcing  →  4) Capture reorder logic, SKU load, fast movers, and stock position  →  5) Review pricing, schemes, competition, and quick-commerce pressure  →  6) Assess credit, funding, working capital, and cash stress  →  7) Check AI adoption, trust, and success metrics.

Sales-team instruction: Ask the structured survey questions first. Use interview probes only after the outlet owner/staff has responded to the structured question. Capture evidence in the observation column before scoring.

column6: Question
column8: Answer Options / Probe Angle

column6: What type of kirana store is this?
column8: Neighborhood kirana; Mini supermarket; General store; Wholesale-retail hybrid; Other

column6: How many years has this outlet been operating?
column8: <1 year; 1–3 years; 4–7 years; 8–15 years; 15+ years

column6: How many people work in this outlet, including family members?
Are you using any device / computer / billing machine for POS or store operations?
column8: No device / manual only; Smartphone only; Basic billing machine; Computer + billing/POS; Mixed setup

column6: How would you rate your digital capability / knowledge for running the store?
column8: 1 Very low → 5 Very confident

column6: How do you enter purchases and sales today, and how do you arrive at profit?
column8: Memory only; Notebook / register; Calculator; Billing / POS report; Excel; Tally / accounting app; Family member / CA support; Not tracked clearly

column6: Walk me through how this store makes money across key categories.
column8: What drives best margin? Fastest cash rotation? Emotionally vs financially important categories? | Signal: Margin-maximizing vs cash-maximizing behavior

column6: How do you decide what to reorder on a normal day or week?
column8: What is memory-based? Distributor-based? Sales-based? | Signal: AI must fit existing reorder heuristics

column6: Which categories contribute most to monthly sales?
column8: Staples; Snacks; Beverages; Dairy; Home care; Personal care; Frozen; Tobacco / impulse; Other

column6: What share of sales is repeat local-customer vs walk-in impulse?
column8: Mostly repeat; More repeat than impulse; Roughly equal; More impulse than repeat; Mostly impulse

column6: How do you currently identify repeat customers / regular households?
column8: Mostly memory; Notebook; Billing / POS; WhatsApp lists; Loyalty / CRM; No formal method

column6: What unmet customer-demand / wishlist signals do you capture?
column8: Asked but unavailable items; Brand / pack-size requests; Price complaints; Competitor availability; Seasonal asks; WhatsApp requests; No formal capture

column6: How do you know what repeat households buy regularly and what they wish you stocked?
column8: Do you track by memory, WhatsApp, billing history, notebook, staff feedback, or customer asks? | Signal: Customer analytics + wishlist capture model

column6: How frequently do you purchase from the market / wholesale source?
column8: Daily; 2–3 times per week; Weekly; Fortnightly; Monthly / only when needed

column6: For the same item, where do you usually buy from or compare before purchase?

column6: How do you decide what to reorder?

column6: How often do you place replenishment orders?
column8: Daily; 2–3 times per week; Weekly; Fortnightly; Monthly / as needed

column6: What usually triggers urgent or last-minute purchases?

column6: Do you have clarity on fast-moving items, and how do you manage them?

column6: Which stock-position views are most useful every day?

column6: Tell me about the last unexpected stockout or demand spike.
column8: What happened? How did you recover? What data would have helped? | Signal: Alert and exception use cases

column6: What are the current challenges affecting the store most?

column6: How frequently do you check or compare quick-commerce platforms (Blinkit, Zepto, BB, Swiggy Instamart) for pricing, assortment, or offers?
column8: Daily; Weekly; Occasionally; Rarely; Never

column6: How many distributor visits do you receive per week?

column8: 1 Do not trust → 5 Trust completely

column6: Which apps do you use regularly for business?

column8: Yes definitely; Probably yes; Not sure; Probably no; Definitely no

column6: Show me how sales and stock data are recorded today.

column6: If this tool works, what business outcome should improve first?
column8: Less stockout? Less dead stock? Better margin? Better turns? | Signal: Pilot KPIs
